entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: General term for the genus of cereal crops discussed in the study

entities.name: GRAIN SORGHUM
entities.description: Cultivated sorghum species used for grain production

entities.name: SORGHUM BICOLOR
entities.description: The scientific name for cultivated grain sorghum

entities.name: SORGHUM ALMUM
entities.description: A wild species of sorghum exhibiting high dormancy

entities.name: SORGHUM INTRANS
entities.description: A wild species of sorghum exhibiting high dormancy

entities.name: SORGHUM HALEPENSE
entities.description: A wild species of sorghum exhibiting high dormancy

entities.name: SORGHUM STIPODIUM
entities.description: A wild species of sorghum exhibiting high dormancy

entities.name: SORGHUM VERTICILLIFLORUM
entities.description: A wild species of sorghum exhibiting high dormancy

entities.name: SEED GERMINATION
entities.description: The process by which a seed develops into a new plant

entities.name: DORMANCY
entities.description: A physiological state that prevents viable seeds from germinating

entities.name: VIABILITY
entities.description: The ability of a seed to germinate under favorable conditions

entities.name: DEGLUMING
entities.description: The process of removing glumes from seeds to facilitate germination

entities.name: LONG-TERM STORAGE
entities.description: The preservation of germplasm for extended periods to maintain genetic diversity

entities.name: GLUMES
entities.description: The outer husks of seeds that can physically or chemically prevent germination

entities.name: GENEBANK
entities.description: A facility used for the storage and preservation of genetic resources

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL GENEBANK STANDARDS
entities.description: Global guidelines for the management and storage of germplasm

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

entities.name: PATANCHERU
entities.description: The specific site of the ICRISAT genebank in India

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the research and genebank are located

relationships.description: Sorghum seeds undergo the process of germination
Dormancy prevents or delays seed germination
Glumes can prevent or delay seed germination
Degluming is used to influence and improve seed germination
Grain sorghum shows dormancy at harvest
Sorghum almum shows high levels of dormancy
Sorghum intrans shows high levels of dormancy
Sorghum halepense shows high levels of dormancy
Sorghum stipodium shows high levels of dormancy
Sorghum verticilliflorum shows high levels of dormancy
Viability levels determine eligibility for long-term storage
Sorghum germplasm is processed for long-term storage
ICRISAT genebank is located in Patancheru
International Genebank Standards guide the process of long-term storage
